summary: S4 SOO redteam workflow is approved with a tracked deviation: direct thin SOO jobs use an explicit framework SHA pin so environment-scoped redteam secrets can be enforced.

questions: Is the reusable-workflow preference explicitly superseded by the stronger environment-scoped secret requirement?
Will one agent path change prove only the matching redteam job runs in GitHub Actions?
Do direct thin SOO jobs keep framework/package execution pinned to an explicit SHA?
Can S5 prove the redteam environment contains staging-only LiveKit, Langfuse, OpenAI, DB, and Slack secrets without printing sensitive values?
Can S5 confirm support-agent-maya emits Langfuse correlation metadata despite using MAYA_LANGFUSE_* internally?
Does write-results plus enforce-threshold stay blocked from trusted live acceptance until S6/S7 DB readiness is proven?
Are no prod/canary triggers present before explicit later phase approval?
Do fork PRs skip secret-backed jobs as intended?
Do operational metrics remain pending until non-stub workflow evidence exists?
Can release readiness be blocked unless PostHog flagging, non-stub E2E evidence, cost guardrail proof, and security reviews are complete?
Are cost, latency, scalability, reliability, API-outage behavior, and bottlenecks measured before each phase is closed?
Will Strategic View triage reviewer findings and vt-agent-redteam cutover into fix-now work versus post-v0 backlog before any final report?
Is the v0.1.0 tag visible and installable for consumers?
Did the package metadata and CLI version agree on 0.1.0?
Is a signed tag required by release policy before SOO consumes this?
Was a real green fixture workflow recorded before closing F6?
Does the reusable workflow preserve manifest validation and stable REDTEAM_RUN_ID?
Which downstream guardrails remain for live secrets, write-results, and schema rollout?
Do PR/deploy/canary modes default to the Langfuse native transcript path?
Does dry-run remain local and safe for fixture validation?
Does non-dry-run fail fast when Langfuse credentials are missing?
Are prompt and response fields redacted before persistence?
Is response_hash computed before redaction for audit stability?
Can expected-behavior allowlists preserve intentionally required spans?
Does P0 remain non-bypassable even with an override?
Are overrides read independently from result writes?
Are transient failures classified below true policy failures?
Does the manifest distinguish full coverage from partial-no-tool-use?
Can scenario_selection exclude unsupported tags without hiding coverage gaps?
Is the coverage state visible before SOO adoption?
